meaning: n.十字形adj.十字形的adv.十字形地vi.交叉往来vt.画十字形于
examples: This was Nahal David, one of four springs that crisscross the oasis.

As manufacturing has moved abroad, more finished goods need to crisscross the country from ports.
The matrix is a crisscross of business units and functions (portrayed as verticals and horizontals).
